codeReview.summary: The feature implementation matches the requested behavior. `website/components/Features.tsx` adds a semantic `#features` section with an h2, six distinct SVG-backed cards, dark slate styling, responsive 1/2/3-column grid classes, and hover glow treatment; `website/app/page.tsx` integrates it in the expected page flow. I did not find correctness or maintainability issues in the shipped feature diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-worker` skill mixes testing guidance: it says component testing uses Vitest, but the mission conventions require `bun:test`. That mismatch can send workers to the wrong test runner unless they cross-check repo context every time.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/moizibnyousaf/projects/mktg/.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:19 says "Component testing with Vitest + React Testing Library", while /Users/moizibnyousaf/.factory/missions/f6087f66-ecf8-4dd2-b2bd-cf52fdf61f16/AGENTS.md:52 says tests use `bun:test` (not jest/vitest).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to deviate from the skill's TDD/clean-tree procedure because the assigned worktree already contained partially implemented feature files and unrelated dirty state. The skill should explicitly document how to handle pre-seeded feature work and dirty repositories.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/moizibnyousaf/.factory/missions/f6087f66-ecf8-4dd2-b2bd-cf52fdf61f16/handoffs/2026-03-13T10-35-12-917Z__features-grid__86a84425-4001-4b9e-b1ac-a8c663a481ca.json:90-104 records `followedProcedure: false`, cites the "Write Tests First (TDD)" deviation because the feature files were already present, and asks for guidance on dirty-tree handling. The skill's TDD instructions live at /Users/moizibnyousaf/projects/mktg/.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:30-33.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo does not have a single source of truth for the website's marketed skill count. Core project docs say 39 skills, while the website copy and this feature spec still say 35, making future marketing edits easy to drift out of sync.

summary: Pass. The features grid commit delivers the requested section, card content, responsive layout, styling, and page integration without review-blocking issues. I noted shared-state gaps around outdated skill-count source-of-truth and frontend-worker guidance inconsistencies, but the shipped feature itself looks correct.
